branch.title: New Branch
branch.fromCommitDescription: Create a new branch from the current commit.

tag.title: New Tag
tag.description: Create a tag from the current commit.

reset.title: Reset Current Branch
reset.footerHint: The current branch will move to the selected commit. You can still recover history later with git reflog.
reset.modeDescription: Each mode keeps or discards the index and working tree differently.

mixed.label: Mixed
mixed.description: Keep working tree changes, reset the index, and continue organizing files before recommitting.

soft.label: Soft
soft.description: Move only the branch pointer and keep both the index and working tree intact.

hard.label: Hard
hard.description: Reset HEAD, the index, and the working tree together. Unsaved local changes will be discarded.

undoCommit.title: Undo Commit
undoCommit.description: Reset to the parent of the current commit and keep the working tree changes.
undoCommit.footerHint: This keeps file content in the working tree, but removes the current commit from branch history.
undoCommit.targetChangeListDescription: Moved files will be restored to the selected changelist, and the original commit message will be copied back into that draft.

deleteCommit.title: Delete Commit
deleteCommit.footerHint: This rewrites the current branch history. Be careful if these commits were already pushed.

commitDetailsPatch.applyDescription: Apply the selected changes from the current commit details into the working tree. When changelists are enabled, you can also choose the target changelist.
commitDetailsPatch.revertDescription: Apply the selected changes from the current commit details into the working tree as a reverse patch. When changelists are enabled, you can also choose the target changelist.
commitDetailsPatch.applyTargetDescription: Applied files will be moved into the selected changelist so they do not fall into the wrong list by default.
commitDetailsPatch.revertTargetDescription: Reverted files will be moved into the selected changelist so you can continue organizing them afterward.

editCommitMessage.title: Edit Commit Message
editCommitMessage.description: Edit commit messages on the current branch history. Non-HEAD commits are applied through an automated rebase.
editCommitMessage.footerHint: Editing a non-HEAD commit triggers a controlled rebase. Review the final message before saving.

remoteEditor.addDescription: Add a new Git remote configuration
remoteEditor.editDescription: Update the name and URLs for remote {{name}}
remoteEditor.pushUrlPlaceholder: Leave blank to reuse the Fetch URL

checkoutRevision.title: Checkout tag or revision
checkoutRevision.description: Enter a tag name or commit hash
checkoutRevision.label: Tag / revision

fixup.title: Create Fixup Commit
fixup.description: Create an automatic commit only from the {{count}} selected changes in the current Git panel. It will not expand to the whole repository.

squashTo.title: Create Squash Commit
squashTo.description: Create an automatic commit only from the {{count}} selected changes in the current Git panel. It will not expand to the whole repository.

squashCommits.title: Squash Commits
squashCommits.description: Squash the selected consecutive commits into one commit. The range must be on the current branch mainline and the working tree must be clean.

deleteChangeList.chooseNextDescription: Before deleting the active changelist, choose another active list to receive the files.
deleteChangeList.title: Delete Changelist
deleteChangeList.descriptionWithTarget: Delete {{count}} changelist(s) ({{names}}). Their files will be moved to the selected target list.
deleteChangeList.descriptionWithoutTarget: Delete {{count}} changelist(s) ({{names}}). Their files will return to the active changelist.

worktree.title: New Worktree
worktree.description: Enter the new worktree path and optional branch name.
worktree.descriptionWithRef: Enter the new worktree path and optional branch name (based on {{ref}}).
worktree.emptyPathError: The worktree path cannot be empty

dateFilter.title: Date Filter
dateFilter.description: Enter a date range (ISO date format, for example 2026-03-05).

errors.showWorkbenchUnsupported: The current host does not support opening GitWorkbench

fetch.title: Fetch Remote Changes
fetch.description: Choose the target repository, remote scope, and extra flags explicitly so fetch does not collapse into a single default action.
fetch.remoteHint: Only applies in Specific Remote mode.
fetch.unshallow: Convert a shallow clone into full history (`--unshallow`)
fetch.noRemotes: This repository does not have any remotes configured yet

defaultRemote.label: Default Remote
defaultRemote.description: Mirror native `git fetch`: prefer the current branch upstream, then fall back to origin.

specificRemote.label: Specific Remote
specificRemote.description: Pick one remote explicitly. Useful for repositories with multiple remotes.

allRemotes.label: All Remotes
allRemotes.description: Fetch every configured remote for the current repository in sequence.

pull.title: Pull
pull.description: Choose a remote and branch to run an explicit pull.

merge.label: Merge
merge.description: Keep the current history and merge remote changes into the current branch.

rebase.label: Rebase
rebase.description: Fetch first, then replay local commits on top of the latest remote state.

options.mergeOnly: This merge option is unavailable in rebase mode.

ffOnly.label: Fast-forward only
ffOnly.description: Allow only fast-forward updates. Fail immediately if a merge commit would be required.

noFf.label: No fast-forward
noFf.description: Keep a merge commit even when fast-forward is possible so the pull stays explicit in history.

squash.label: Squash
squash.description: Flatten remote changes into working tree changes without creating a merge commit immediately.

noCommit.label: Do not auto-commit
noCommit.description: Stop after merge and leave changes in the staging area for manual review.

noVerify.label: Skip hooks
noVerify.description: Skip pre-merge / pre-rebase hooks when the current Git version supports it.
noVerify.unsupported: The current Git version does not support `git pull --no-verify`.

interactiveRebase.title: Interactive Rebase
interactiveRebase.description: {{count}} commits in total, ranging from {{from}} to the current HEAD.

autosquash.title: Autosquash commits detected
autosquash.message: The plan contains fixup!/squash!/amend! commits. The in-app editor always shows the real replay order and runs with `--no-autosquash`.

updateRefs.title: rebase.updateRefs detected
updateRefs.message: The current Git config enables `rebase.updateRefs`. The in-app result still follows real Git execution, but related refs are rewritten during the rebase.

interactiveRebase.discardChangesConfirm: The current interactive rebase plan has changed. Discard these edits?
interactiveRebase.orderHint: Commits are shown from oldest to newest. Moving entries changes the final replay order directly.
interactiveRebase.squashPlaceholder: Leave empty to use Git's default squash message
interactiveRebase.squashHint: If left empty, Git generates the default squash message from the current rebase state. Fill it manually or use Fill Suggested Message to pin the final message.
interactiveRebase.empty: There are no editable commits right now

actions.attachTargetMissing: There is no previous commit available to attach to

reason.mergeCommit: The selected commit is a merge commit, and in-app interactive rebase is not supported.
reason.nonLinearHistory: The selected commit range is not a linear first-parent history, so in-app interactive rebase is not supported.
reason.targetOutsideHead: The selected commit is no longer on HEAD history. Refresh the log and try again.
reason.unexpectedHash: The commit history has changed. Refresh the log and try again.
reason.unresolvedHash: Failed to read the current commit chain. Refresh the log and try again.
reason.detachedHead: Interactive rebase editor is unavailable in detached HEAD.
reason.commitNotFound: Failed to read the target commit. Refresh the log and try again.

fallback.started: The current history cannot use the in-app rebase editor, so a traditional interactive rebase was started in an in-app terminal. Return to the Git panel and refresh after it finishes.
fallback.openTerminalFailed: Failed to start a traditional interactive rebase in the in-app terminal. Try again later or use an external terminal.
fallback.prepareFailed: Failed to prepare the traditional interactive rebase fallback

shelfRestore.title: Restore Shelved Change
shelfRestore.description: Choose which files to restore and decide whether applied content should be removed from the shelf after the restore succeeds.

files.title: Files to Restore
files.empty: This shelved change does not contain a restorable file list.

removeApplied.title: Remove Successfully Applied Content
removeApplied.description: When enabled, restored files are removed from the shelf. When disabled, the original record stays available for later reuse.

rollbackViewer.empty: There are no local changes to display right now.
rollbackViewer.summary: {{selected}} / {{total}} selected. Rollback only applies to the checked files and leaves all other local changes untouched.

fallbacks.binary: Binary content is not supported in the in-app preview.
fallbacks.tooLarge: The file is too large for the in-app preview.

toolbar.focusManual: Focused on block {{index}}, manual review required
toolbar.focusHint: The left and right panes are read-only sources. The center pane is editable. Apply or ignore blocks before clicking Apply.

hints.allResolved: All change blocks are handled. You can apply the current result now.
hints.unresolvedChangesOnly: {{count}} change blocks are still unresolved, but none of them are real conflicts.

prompts.resolveWithPending: {{changes}} unresolved blocks remain, including {{conflicts}} conflicts. Continuing writes the current result back to disk and runs git add. Continue?
prompts.acceptSourceWithLabel: The current result has been modified. Continue to replace the full result with {{label}} and close the merge viewer?
prompts.acceptSource: The current result has been modified. Continue to replace the full result with that side and close the merge viewer?

currentBlock.title: Current Block
currentBlock.summaryEmpty: There are no pending blocks. You can still review the result pane and click Apply to finish.

selectedBlockHint.none: There is no pending block. You can still inspect the result pane and click Apply when ready.
selectedBlockHint.modified: This block has been edited manually. Auto handling is disabled to avoid overwriting your manual result.
selectedBlockHint.oneSideApplied: One side has already been applied to this block. You can still apply the other side, restore the base, or keep both sides.
selectedBlockHint.manual: This block needs a manual comparison before you decide.

viewSource.title: View Source
viewSource.description: This only changes focus and visible line ranges. It does not modify content.

baseDescription.default: Base = the shared original text before both sides started editing.
baseDescription.missing: This block has no base. Usually both sides added content here, or the shared original was empty.
baseDescription.withBase: Base = the shared original text before both sides started editing. Restoring the base puts this block back to that shared original.

processCurrentBlock.title: Process Current Block
processCurrentBlock.description: These buttons only affect the selected block and never replace the whole file.

pendingList.title: Pending Blocks
pendingList.description: Click any item to jump to the matching block.
pendingList.empty: There are no pending blocks left. You can continue reviewing and apply the result.

autoResolution.auto: Automatically merge compatible changes from both sides

actions.applyNonConflicted: Apply non-conflicting changes from this side
actions.applyCurrentBlock: Use this side for the current block
actions.keepBoth: Keep both sides in current block
actions.ignoreOurs: Ignore left side in current block
actions.ignoreTheirs: Ignore right side in current block
actions.resolveWithSource: Use this side for entire result

multipleFileMerge.title: Conflict Files

scopeHint.reverseSides: This repository is using reversed conflict-side semantics. Left/right sources and accept actions have been corrected to match the current mapping.
scopeHint.default: Browse conflicted files on the left and use the right side to accept changes, merge, or open externally.

summary.batch: Bulk actions will apply to {{count}} unresolved files
summary.single: When nothing is checked, bulk actions apply to the currently selected unresolved file
summary.resolved: The current selection is a resolved file. You can still open it to review the result.
summary.prompt: Select an unresolved file, or check multiple files before applying a bulk action

details.primaryActionDisabled: This file cannot run the primary action right now.
details.primaryActionExternal: Primary action: {{label}} (hand off to an external IDE or the system default app)
details.resolvedFile: This file has been removed from the unresolved list, but it remains in the shared state source and can still be opened to review the current result.
details.canOpenMerge: This file can still continue into in-app merge.
details.cannotOpenMerge: This file does not support in-app merge. Hand it off to an external IDE or the system default app.
details.batchApplied: With {{count}} unresolved files checked, Accept Side Changes will write the changes back in batch and run `git add` automatically.
details.noSelectionHint: No file is selected. If all unresolved files are cleared, the dialog will auto-close; resolved files will still share the same state source as the commit panel and status bar.
details.operationInProgress: The repository is still in progress for {{badge}}. After closing, the top status bar will keep the continue / abort entry.

multipleFileMerge.empty: There are no conflicted files to display.
multipleFileMerge.reopenDescription: The current repository still has unresolved conflicts. Return to the unified resolver and keep going.
multipleFileMerge.openTargetRepoSuccess: The target repository has been opened. You can continue resolving its conflicts.

descriptions.publishedCommits: The current branch contains commits that have already been published to a remote. Continuing the rebase rewrites that history and may affect collaborators.
descriptions.mergeCommits: The current branch contains merge commits. Continuing the rebase may change the historical structure of those merge points.

details.publishedCommits: At least {{published}} of the {{total}} commits to rebase have already appeared in remote references.
details.publishedCommitsWithoutTotal: At least {{published}} commits to rebase have already appeared in remote references.
details.mergeCommits: {{count}} merge commit(s) were detected. You can switch to Merge update or explicitly confirm that the rebase should continue.

titles.noFastForward: Push rejected. Sync the remote first

descriptions.staleInfo: The remote reference changed and the current lease protection has expired. Use a normal force push only if you still intend to overwrite the remote.
descriptions.rejectedOther: The remote rejected this push. Resolve the remote policy or server hook restriction and try again.
descriptions.noFastForwardWithUpstream: The remote branch {{upstream}} is ahead of the current branch. Update first and retry the push, or switch to Force with Lease if you intend to overwrite the remote.
descriptions.noFastForwardWithRemote: The remote {{remote}}/{{branch}} is ahead of the current branch. Update first and retry the push, or switch to Force with Lease if you intend to overwrite the remote.

force.title: Continue force push
force.description: Switching to a normal force push removes lease protection. Use it only when you are sure the remote reference should be overwritten.

cancel.title: Cancel
cancel.description: Keep the current state and stop pushing. You can pull, inspect remote history, and decide later whether to overwrite.

policy.title: Handle remote restrictions first
policy.description: This rejection usually comes from branch protection, a server hook, or a platform policy. Remove the remote restriction before retrying.

cancel.title: Close
cancel.description: Keep the current commit state for now, then retry the push after the remote rules are satisfied.

update.title: Update before pushing
update.description: Choose Merge or Rebase. The update flow will enter the formal Update Session and retry the push after it succeeds.

forceWithLease.title: Force with lease
forceWithLease.description: Use this only when the remote changes should be overwritten by the current commit. Lease protection is still preserved to avoid blind force pushes.

cancel.title: Cancel
cancel.description: Keep the current push dialog state. You can adjust the update method, target branch, or close it later.

updateReset.title: Reset to Remote Tracking Branch
updateReset.description: This action updates the current repository through the Reset path and may overwrite working tree content. Confirm before continuing.
updateReset.warning: This is not a normal Update option. It only runs a one-off Reset for the current repository and does not overwrite the saved Merge / Rebase defaults.

branchCompare.titleFiles: Choose another branch to compare files
branchCompare.titleCommits: Choose another branch to compare commits
branchCompare.descriptionFiles: Show file differences between {{target}} and another branch
branchCompare.descriptionCommits: Show commit differences between {{target}} and another branch

branchCompareFiles.title: Compare Files
branchCompareFiles.description: {{count}} files in total. Click a file to open the diff.
branchCompareFiles.empty: There are no file differences in the current comparison scope.

remoteManager.title: Remote Manager
remoteManager.emptyInline: No remotes are configured for this repository yet
remoteManager.empty: This repository does not have any remote configurations. Use Add Remote to enter the fetch / push addresses.

updateCommitRanges.title: View Updated Commits
updateCommitRanges.description: This update touched multiple repositories. Choose the commit range you want to inspect.

updateOptions.title: Update Options
updateOptions.description: Save the default Update Project method, local change preservation policy, and linked repository scope.
updateOptions.savePolicyHint: `stash` writes to Git stash entries, while `shelve` writes to the shared saved-changes area. Both continue through the same restore entry point.

merge.title: Merge
merge.description: Always update the current branch with Merge.

rebase.title: Rebase
rebase.description: Always update the current branch with Rebase.

stash.title: Stash
stash.description: Use Git stash entries to save and restore local changes through the shared viewer.

shelve.title: Shelve
shelve.description: Use the shared shelve storage to save and restore local changes.

summary.detached: Detached HEAD / switch to an updatable branch
summary.executionPrefix: If Update Project runs now, it will use

scope.title: Default Multi-Repo Scope
scope.summary: {{includedCount}} repositories are included by default; {{linkedCount}} linked repositories are configured.

includeNestedRoots.title: Scan regular nested repositories
includeNestedRoots.description: Submodules always participate in dependency ordering. When enabled, regular nested Git repositories under the current and linked repos are also scanned.

current.title: Current repository only
current.description: Only update the current repository and its submodules by default.

linked.title: Include linked repositories
linked.description: Include linked repositories in Update Project and allow the default skip rules to exclude some roots.

linkedRepos.title: Linked Repositories
linkedRepos.description: Manage the independent repository roots that should join Update Project in linked mode by default.
linkedRepos.empty: No linked repositories yet. Add one here if multiple standalone repositories should join the default Update scope.
linkedRepos.chooseFailed: Failed to choose a linked repository folder

preview.title: Default Scope Preview
preview.description: The preview is calculated from the real repository graph, submodules, and skip rules instead of a static frontend guess.
preview.refreshFailed: Failed to refresh the multi-repository scope preview
preview.dirtyHint: This preview is based on the previous scan. Refresh once after changing linked repos, skip rules, or nested scan settings before saving.
preview.empty: There are no repositories to preview right now.
preview.requestedSkipReason: Skipped by the default scope configuration

updateRuntimeScope.title: Choose This Update Scope
updateRuntimeScope.description: This only affects the current Update Project run. Saved defaults and multi-repo settings are not changed.

summary.selection: {{count}} repositories selected. Confirming writes the explicit scope into the runtime payload without changing saved defaults.

risk.title: Execution Risk
risk.description: This highlights detached / skipped / submodule differences that directly affect multi-repository Update behavior.
risk.detachedNote: Regular repositories in Detached HEAD are usually skipped during preflight. Detached submodules may either update through the parent repository or by a standalone updater.

repositories.title: Repositories
repositories.description: The current repository is always included. Linked repos, nested repos, and submodules can be toggled just for this run.
repositories.detachedNote: This root is currently in Detached HEAD and may be skipped or redirected to a special submodule update path at runtime.

skipped.title: Skipped This Time
skipped.description: These repositories are not entering the execution root list because of repository graph or tracking-branch resolution results.

titles.restoreFromRevision: Restoring from revision will overwrite local changes

localChangesOverwritten.merge: Local changes would be overwritten by Merge
localChangesOverwritten.reset: Local changes would be overwritten by Reset
localChangesOverwritten.checkout: Local changes would be overwritten by Checkout
localChangesOverwritten.cherryPick: Local changes would be overwritten by Cherry-pick

untrackedOverwritten.merge: Untracked files would be overwritten by Merge
untrackedOverwritten.reset: Untracked files would be overwritten by Reset
untrackedOverwritten.checkout: Untracked files would be overwritten by Checkout
untrackedOverwritten.cherryPick: Untracked files would be overwritten by Cherry-pick

mergeConflict.merge: The repository is already in a Merge conflict state. Resolve the conflicts and finish or abort the Merge before continuing.
mergeConflict.cherryPick: The repository is already in a Cherry-pick conflict state. Resolve the conflicts and then continue or abort the Cherry-pick.

descriptions.restoreFromRevision: The target file has local modifications. Continuing overwrites the current local content.

localChangesOverwritten.merge: Local changes in these files would be overwritten by this Merge. Commit, stash, or preserve local changes before retrying.
localChangesOverwritten.reset: Local changes in these files would be overwritten by this Reset. When possible the system protects non-conflicting edits automatically, but if these files are still listed, commit, stash, or handle them manually before retrying.
localChangesOverwritten.checkout: Local changes in these files would be overwritten by this Checkout. Commit, stash, or retry with Smart Checkout first.
localChangesOverwritten.cherryPick: Local changes in these files would be overwritten by this Cherry-pick. Commit, stash, or retry after preserving local changes.

untrackedOverwritten.merge: These untracked files would be overwritten by this Merge. Move, delete, or add them to version control before retrying.
untrackedOverwritten.reset: These untracked files would be overwritten by this Reset. Move, delete, or add them to version control before retrying.
untrackedOverwritten.checkout: These untracked files would be overwritten by this Checkout. Move, delete, or add them to version control before retrying.
untrackedOverwritten.cherryPick: These untracked files would be overwritten by this Cherry-pick. Move, delete, or add them to version control before retrying.

summary.withFiles: {{count}} file(s) must be handled before this {{operation}} can continue.
summary.noFiles: This issue must be handled before this {{operation}} can continue.

smartOperation.emptyFiles: No file list is attached to this issue. Resolve the repository state first and then continue.

description.shelve: Shelve local changes first, then try to restore them after checkout finishes.
description.stash: Stash local changes first, then try to restore them after checkout finishes.

retryWithShelve.label: Shelve Then Retry
retryWithShelve.description: Shelve local changes first, then auto-restore them after the operation succeeds and retry.

retryWithStash.label: Stash Then Retry
retryWithStash.description: Stash local changes first, then auto-restore them after the operation succeeds and retry.

fixTrackedBranch.title: Fix Tracking Branch and Continue
fixTrackedBranch.empty: There are no tracked branch issues to fix right now.
fixTrackedBranch.setTracked: Also write the Git tracking branch config. If unchecked, the change only applies to this Update Project run.
fixTrackedBranch.unsupported: This issue cannot be fixed automatically in this dialog. Switch to a local branch and configure the upstream first.
fixTrackedBranch.resetHint: After fixing the tracked branch, this Reset run continues without changing the saved Merge / Rebase default.

description.multiRoot: Some repositories are missing a usable upstream branch. Confirm each repository before continuing Update Project.
description.singleRoot: The current branch is missing a usable upstream branch. Confirm the remote branch before continuing Update Project.

messages.remoteMissing: The upstream branch {{upstream}} does not exist locally or is no longer valid.
messages.noTrackedBranch: The current branch {{branch}} does not have a remote upstream configured.

continueMethod.title: Continue With
continueMethod.merge: Apply the fix and continue with Merge.
continueMethod.rebase: Apply the fix and continue with Rebase.

summary.title: {{files}} files were updated across {{commits}} commits
summary.filteredCommits: The log view is focused on the primary repository range and can show {{count}} commits.
summary.skippedRoots: {{count}} additional repositories were skipped. Expand the details to review the reasons.

skippedReasons.requested: Skipped by the current scope configuration
skippedReasons.detachedHead: The repository is in Detached HEAD
skippedReasons.noTrackedBranch: No usable upstream branch is configured
skippedReasons.remoteMissing: The upstream branch does not exist locally or is no longer valid
skippedReasons.updatedByParent: Already updated recursively by the parent repository

running.waitingForSnapshot: The main process started the update flow and is waiting for the first session snapshot.
running.multiRootHint: This is a multi-repository Update Session. Each root shows its live phase and closing status below.
running.singleRootHint: This is a single-repository Update Session.

noRepo.title: No Git repository detected
noRepo.description: This directory has not been initialized as a Git repository yet. You can run `git init` here or create a repository from the button below.

splitter.main: Drag to resize the left and right panes; middle-click to collapse or expand the left side
splitter.bottom: Drag to resize the height; middle-click to collapse or expand
splitter.bottomPanel: Middle-click to collapse or expand the bottom panel

updateInfo.pathFilterDescription: Enter a directory or file path (relative to the repository root).
updateInfo.title: {{count}} files updated

console.title: Git Console
console.pausedNotice: Git Console auto refresh is paused. Copy the log first, then click Resume to keep tracking live output.
console.empty: No logs yet. Git commands and output will appear after you run Git actions.

conflicts.title: Git Conflicts
conflicts.dialogDescription: Review the current repository's conflict files in one place with table-style merge/open, directory grouping, and the resolved-conflict state shared with the commit panel.
conflicts.operationProblemDescription: This repository still has unresolved conflicts. Finish handling them before continuing.
conflicts.restoreSavedChangesTitle: Conflicts found while restoring saved changes
conflicts.restoreSavedChangesDescription: Please resolve the conflicts caused by restoring local changes first.
conflicts.resolveBeforeCommitDescription: Unresolved conflicts still exist in the current commit scope. Finish conflict handling before committing again.
conflicts.noEntries: There are no conflict files available to handle right now.
conflicts.tableLoadFailed: Failed to load the conflicts table
conflicts.contentLoadFailed: Failed to load the conflict content
conflicts.writeResultFailed: Failed to write the conflict result
conflicts.markResolvedFailed: Failed to mark the conflict as resolved
conflicts.rollbackResolvedFailed: Failed to roll back the resolved conflict
conflicts.strategyDescription: The conflicts panel is driven by the status refresh chain and automatically appears or hides with staging-area changes and repository mapping changes.
conflicts.openUnifiedDescription: The standalone conflicts panel and the unified resolver share the same conflict state source.
conflicts.empty: There are no conflict files to handle right now.
conflicts.resolutionConflictDetected: A conflict was detected after applying the selected changes in commit details; you can continue handling each conflicted file here.

worktrees.title: Worktrees
worktrees.empty: There are no extra worktrees yet. Create one here and it will appear in this panel, with NEW markers and dismissal memory.
worktrees.helpOpenFailed: Failed to open the worktree help

updateOptions.saved: Saved Update Project options: {{updateMethod}}; the save-changes policy is now {{policy}}

diff.noFileSwitch: No file group selection is available
diff.partialHint: The selected lines match {{lineCount}} lines / {{hunkCount}} hunks
diff.partialGuide: You can click the diff center seam and line checkboxes, or select changed lines first and then include or exclude them in bulk
diff.clickToOpenNote: Clicking a file no longer switches the diff automatically. Use Enter, double-click, right-click "Show Diff", or re-enable the details preview.
diff.exportUnsupported: Patch export is not supported in the current view

push.pushRejectedToUpdateFlow: Enter the update decision flow when push is rejected
push.previewLoadFailed: Failed to load the push preview
push.pushTagsDescription: Select the push remote for tag {{name}}
push.synced: Local branch is synchronized with the remote branch

misc.changeListManyFilesDescription: This node contains {{count}} files. Because the list is large, it is opened through the browse dialog.

console.loadFailed: Failed to load the Git console
console.copyFailed: Failed to copy the Git console log
console.clearFailed: Failed to clear the Git console

changelist.saveCommitDraftFailed: Failed to save the changelist commit draft
changelist.clearCommitDraftsFailed: Failed to clear the changelist commit draft
changelist.activateFailed: Failed to set the active changelist
changelist.targetNotFound: The target changelist was not found

patch.copyFailed: The patch was created, but copying it failed

feedback.conflictEnteredOperation: {{action}} hit a conflict. The repository has entered {{operation}} and can now continue or abort.
feedback.enteredOperation: {{action}} did not finish automatically. The repository has entered {{operation}} and can now continue or abort.

branch.deleteRemoteGenericFailed: Failed to delete the remote branch
branch.forceDeletedWithUnmerged: Branch '{{name}}' was force-deleted. Unmerged commits were detected; use the actions below to restore the branch or inspect the affected range.
branch.invalidRemoteBranchForDelete: The remote branch format is invalid, or deleting the remote HEAD ref is not supported
branch.checkedOutWorktreeNotFound: The checked-out worktree for this branch was not found

historyRewrite.undoDeleteCommitFailed: Failed to undo deleting the commit
historyRewrite.deleteCommitUndone: The deleted commit has been restored
historyRewrite.deleteCommitUndoneDetail: The commit history before deletion has been restored
historyRewrite.undoCommitMoveWarningFallback: Moving changes to the changelist after undoing the commit failed

leftPanel.middleClickToggle: Middle click: collapse/expand the left commit panel

disabled.notRepo: The current directory is not a Git repository
disabled.busy: A Git operation is currently in progress
disabled.noRemotes: This repository has no available remotes
disabled.pullDetached: Pull is not supported in detached HEAD state
disabled.noRollbackSelection: There are no tracked changes available to roll back

notices.cancelRequestFailed: Failed to send the cancel request
notices.updateRequestNotCancelable: The current update request has already finished or cannot be canceled

operationState.cherryPickResolved: All conflicts are resolved. Submit your changes now to finish the current cherry-pick.
operationState.resolveConflictsDescriptionForCherryPick: Review and resolve all conflicted files in the current repository. When everything is resolved, submit your changes to finish the current cherry-pick.
operationState.resolveConflictsDescription: Review and resolve all conflicted files in the current repository. When everything is resolved, you can continue or abort the current Git operation.

rebasing.title: The current repository is in Rebase

merging.title: The current repository is in Merge

grafting.title: The current repository is in Cherry-pick

reverting.title: The current repository is in Revert

hints.unresolved: There are still {{count}} files with unresolved conflicts. Please finish them in the commit panel or an external tool first.
hints.resolved: {{count}} conflict files have been resolved. You can now continue or abort the current operation.
hints.clean: There are no unresolved conflict files in this repository. You can continue or abort directly.

operationState.continueDisabled: There are still unresolved conflicts, so continuing is temporarily disabled

feedback.continueConflict: A conflict happened again while continuing {{operation}}. The repository remains in progress.
feedback.continueRunning: {{operation}} has continued, and the repository remains in progress.
